page: Amazon Web Services c5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz, the CPU includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results position the c5.2xlarge in the average performance tier across most compute and memory operations. The instance achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1175.0 and a multi-core score of 4531.0, with a Passmark CPU score of 8888.09. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only cached small block speed of 437.77 GB/sec, which drops to 50.41 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench image processing show weaker performance, falling into the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Additionally, LLM inference on a medium 7B model is limited, yielding a poor prompt processing speed of 11.09 tokens/sec and a weak text generation speed of 6.68 tokens/sec, though small 135M model inference remains average.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory ratio designed for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention. This profile is suitable for workloads such as batch processing, distributed analytics, web serving, and moderate scientific modeling.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the c5.2xlarge in the average tier for most tasks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 4531.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 8888.09, though it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and larger LLM inference tasks. This server is suited for CPU-intensive workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and software compilation.
